key_insight: The paper decomposes unbounded PDE domains into near-field and far-field regions, using randomized neural subnetworks plus linear least-squares output fitting to handle each regime efficiently.
engineering_relevance: This offers a flexible alternative to artificial boundary truncation for large-scale PDE simulation in open or exterior domains, especially when local detail and far-field decay must both be captured.
method: Assign separate randomized subnetworks to near-field and far-field spatial regimes.
Enforce coupling through boundary and interface conditions.
Solve only the output-layer coefficients via Petrov--Galerkin or collocation least-squares systems.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on Poisson and time-dependent Schrödinger equations, alongside a conditional approximation theorem and error decomposition.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the domain split, interface conditions, and quadrature/least-squares setup are chosen for a given geometry and PDE.
paper_title: Domain-Decomposed Randomized Neural Networks for Partial Differential Equations in Unbounded Domains
